content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real correctness issues in how nearby-work data feeds planned-elsewhere routing. One trusts summary status too early; the other trusts prompt-reviewed nearby-work text too much.

## Votes

- `fr-11-6582129e`: Accept
  The code only prefetches direct relation detail when the summary/title data is sparse, but nearby rendering chooses status from detail first and otherwise falls back to summary or relation status. That leaves a real gap where a non-sparse direct dependency/dependent can render as open from stale list data even if its detail is closed. Sibling and related-work discovery have the inverse problem: summary-closed candidates are filtered out before a detail fetch can prove they are actually open, so valid nearby beads can disappear permanently. Because Nearby work later feeds planned-elsewhere routing, this is a correctness bug, not just cosmetic drift.
- `fr-11-2951dc45`: Accept
  Prompt review currently enforces only contract shape plus verbatim AGENTS guidance; it does not preserve the body of the Nearby work section. The drift check revalidates headings/order only, so a refiner can replace nearby bullets without being rejected. Final review then builds its planned-elsewhere allowlist directly from the resulting prompt text, so hallucinated nearby IDs become trusted routing targets and real nearby IDs can be removed from the allowlist. Since planned-elsewhere findings do not trigger restart, that can incorrectly suppress restart-worthy issues.
